test.//: These tests load the REAL Rust artifacts at runtime — the FFI cdylib (backend-ffi/store/conformance) and the wasm module (backend-wasm) — by file path, not by import, so nx sees no project-graph edge to lenke-core. Without `rustCore` in the inputs the cache would serve a stale pass after a Rust-only change (the native package's own files are unchanged). `dependsOn` rebuilds both artifacts first so an isolated `nx test` never runs against a stale/missing binary.

build:rust.//: Manual dev convenience that shells out to cargo. Its inferred cache key is this package's files, NOT the Rust source it compiles, so caching it would serve a stale cdylib. cargo is incremental anyway — never cache it. The canonical, correctly-keyed builders are lenke-core:build and lenke-core:build-wasm.
